mapping.a_element: cortical surface as the underlying curved manifold
mapping.b_element: positive unit sphere as the underlying curved manifold
angular variation captured via eigenanalysis of a second-moment matrix of tangent departures

mapping.a_element: using manifold geometry to regularize the estimate
mapping.b_element: using geodesic/tangent-space structure (anchored to a reference direction) to constrain the estimate

brief_justification: The overlap is only the generic 'geometry-aware spectral method on a curved manifold'; the actual driving machinery differs. Paper A solves an optimal-transport coupling that aligns two subjects' functional data (a matching/transport problem regularized by Laplace-Beltrami eigenmodes), whereas Paper B performs low-rank dimension reduction of a single extremal angular law by reducing a geodesic loss to eigen-decomposition of a second-moment matrix (a geodesic-PCA problem). Transport/alignment between distributions is not the same governing process as covariance eigen-decomposition, so no role-for-role mapping of the core mechanism holds.
